title: Power Failure
shortDescription: A power interruption occurred during a drying cycle.
fullDescription: Error code PF indicates the dryer lost power during an active cycle. When power is restored, the dryer displays PF to notify you the cycle was interrupted. You need to restart the cycle.

fixSteps.title: Restart cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Start button to resume or select a new cycle and press Start. PF is informational and clears when you restart.

fixSteps.title: Check circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: If there was no general power outage, check the dryer's circuit breaker in the electrical panel. A 240V dryer uses a double-pole breaker.

fixSteps.title: Inspect power cord
fixSteps.instruction: If PF occurs repeatedly, unplug the dryer and inspect the power cord prongs and the terminal block connection inside the dryer for loose or burnt connections.

fixSteps.title: Check wall outlet
fixSteps.instruction: Test the wall outlet voltage with a multimeter. Electric dryers should read 240V between the two hot terminals.

whenToCallPro: If PF occurs repeatedly without a known power outage, have an electrician check the circuit.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore dryer PF error power failure fix

faq.q: Will PF go away on its own?
faq.a: PF stays displayed until you press a button. It is a notification, not a fault that needs repair.

faq.q: Are my clothes dry after PF?
faq.a: The cycle was interrupted, so clothes are likely still damp. Run the cycle again.

faq.q: Does PF damage the dryer?
faq.a: Occasional PF events from power outages do not damage the dryer.

title: Main Control Board EEPROM Error
shortDescription: The main control board's EEPROM memory has a read/write error.
fullDescription: Error code F01 indicates the main electronic control board cannot properly read or write to its onboard EEPROM memory. This memory stores cycle parameters and configuration data. Without functioning memory, the dryer cannot operate properly.

causes.cause: Failed EEPROM chip on control board

symptoms: F01 or F1 displayed on panel
May not respond to any buttons

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 10 minutes. An EEPROM error may be a transient fault that clears with a full power cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the main control board and reseat all wire harness connectors. A poor connection can sometimes cause memory read errors.

fixSteps.title: Inspect board for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Look for visually damaged components on the control board - burnt areas, swollen capacitors, or cracked solder joints.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If F01 persists after a power reset, replace the main electronic control board.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable swapping the control board.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore dryer F01 error control board EEPROM fix

faq.q: Can F01 be fixed without replacing the board?
faq.a: Rarely. A power reset may clear a transient error, but a persistent F01 typically means the EEPROM chip has failed and the board must be replaced.

faq.q: Is F01 a fire risk?
faq.a: No, F01 prevents the dryer from operating at all, so there is no heating or fire risk.

faq.q: Can I use aftermarket boards?
faq.a: Aftermarket boards may work but compatibility is not guaranteed. OEM boards are recommended for reliability.

title: Communication Failure Between Boards
shortDescription: The main control board and user interface board have lost communication.
fullDescription: Error code F70 indicates a serial communication failure between the main electronic control board and the user interface board. The two boards cannot exchange data, preventing the dryer from operating.
symptoms: Dryer does not respond to buttons
Display may be blank or garbled

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes. Communication glitches may clear with a full reset.

fixSteps.title: Reseat communication cable
fixSteps.instruction: Open the control console. Locate and disconnect the ribbon cable or wire harness connecting the two boards. Reconnect firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect cable
fixSteps.instruction: Check the cable for tears, kinks, or corrosion at the connector pins.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If the cable is good, replace the UI board first (more common failure). If that does not help, replace the main board.

whenToCallPro: If reseating cables does not fix the issue and you cannot determine which board has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore dryer F70 error communication fix

faq.q: How do I know which board is bad?
faq.a: If the display works but does not respond to commands, the main board is more likely at fault. If the display is dead, the UI board is more likely.

faq.q: Can dryer vibration cause F70?
faq.a: Yes. Vibration can loosen the cable connector over time. Ensure the dryer is level.

faq.q: Are Kenmore dryer boards the same as Whirlpool?
faq.a: If your Kenmore model number starts with 110, it is Whirlpool-manufactured and uses Whirlpool parts.

title: Exhaust Thermistor Failure
shortDescription: The exhaust temperature thermistor has failed or is reading out of range.
fullDescription: Error code F22 indicates the outlet (exhaust) temperature thermistor is providing readings outside the expected range. The control board uses this sensor to monitor drying temperatures and cannot safely control heat without valid readings.
symptoms: Dryer may not heat or may overheat
Auto-dry cycles end too early or too late

fixSteps.title: Locate thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. The exhaust thermistor is usually located on or near the exhaust duct inside the dryer housing.

fixSteps.title: Check connection
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the thermistor wire connector. Ensure it is firmly seated and not corroded.

fixSteps.title: Test thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor and measure resistance. At room temperature (72F), expect approximately 10K-12K ohms. An open (OL) or zero reading means it is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw. Install the new thermistor and reconnect the wiring.

whenToCallPro: Only if replacing the thermistor does not clear the error.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore dryer F22 error exhaust thermistor fix

faq.q: What is a thermistor?
faq.a: A thermistor is a temperature-sensitive resistor whose resistance changes with temperature, allowing the control board to monitor air temperature electronically.

faq.q: Can I run the dryer with F22?
faq.a: No. Without accurate temperature monitoring, the dryer could overheat. Fix the thermistor before use.

faq.q: Is F22 a fire risk?
faq.a: The error itself prevents operation, which is the safety response. However, a failed temperature sensor that reads low could cause overheating before the error is detected.

title: Inlet Thermistor Failure
shortDescription: The inlet (supply air) temperature thermistor has failed.
fullDescription: Error code F23 indicates the inlet temperature thermistor is not providing valid readings. This sensor monitors the temperature of air entering the drum from the heater. Without this feedback, the control board cannot regulate heat properly.

causes.cause: Heat damage to thermistor or wiring

symptoms: Dryer will not start on some models

fixSteps.title: Locate inlet thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. The inlet thermistor is near the heater housing or air intake to the drum.

fixSteps.title: Test thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test resistance. Should be approximately 10K-12K ohms at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check thermistor wiring for heat damage since it is near the heater assembly. Melted insulation can cause shorts or opens.

fixSteps.title: Replace thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Install the new thermistor. Route wiring away from direct heater contact.

whenToCallPro: Only if the thermistor tests good but the error persists.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore dryer F23 error inlet thermistor fix

faq.q: Is F23 the same fix as F22?
faq.a: Very similar. Both involve thermistor replacement. F22 is the exhaust thermistor and F23 is the inlet thermistor. They may use the same or different part numbers.

faq.q: Can I use the dryer on no-heat?
faq.a: Some models may allow air-dry (no heat) cycles even with F23, but this is model-dependent.

faq.q: Can dryer sheet residue affect thermistors?
faq.a: Dryer sheet residue can coat thermistors and affect their readings, though this is uncommon. Occasional cleaning with rubbing alcohol can help.

title: Door Open Error
shortDescription: The dryer detects the door is open or cannot confirm it is closed.
fullDescription: Error code dE on LG-manufactured Kenmore dryers (796 series) indicates the door switch does not detect the door is closed. The dryer will not operate with the door open for safety reasons.

causes.cause: Control board door switch input failure

symptoms: Door appears closed but dryer does not recognize it
Dryer stops mid-cycle if door switch is intermittent

fixSteps.title: Close door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open and close the door firmly until you hear the latch click securely.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the door latch for bending, wear, or damage. The latch should engage smoothly with the strike plate.

fixSteps.title: Test door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer. Access the door switch behind the front panel. Test continuity with the switch actuated and unactuated.

fixSteps.title: Replace door switch
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch does not properly change state, replace it. Remove the retaining clip or screws, disconnect wiring, and install the new switch.

whenToCallPro: Only if the switch and latch are both good but dE persists.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore LG dryer dE error door switch fix

faq.q: Is the door switch an expensive part?
faq.a: No, door switches are typically $8-$20 and are one of the easiest dryer parts to replace.

faq.q: Can a worn latch cause intermittent dE?
faq.a: Yes. A worn latch may hold the door closed but not firmly engage the switch, causing the dryer to stop intermittently.

faq.q: Does dE apply to both gas and electric dryers?
faq.a: Yes. The door switch system is the same regardless of the heat source.

title: Thermistor Error
shortDescription: A temperature thermistor in the dryer has failed or is reading abnormally.
fullDescription: Error code tE on LG-manufactured Kenmore dryers indicates one of the temperature thermistors is providing abnormal readings. The control board cannot safely manage heating without accurate temperature feedback from all sensors.
symptoms: Dryer will not heat or will not start
May appear at beginning of cycle

fixSteps.title: Identify which thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: LG-made Kenmore dryers may have multiple thermistors. Check both the inlet and exhaust thermistors for issues.

fixSteps.title: Test each thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect each thermistor and measure resistance. At room temperature, expect 10K-12K ohms. Replace any showing open or zero.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Clean any corroded connectors with electrical contact cleaner. Ensure firm connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the defective thermistor. Ensure the mounting is secure and wiring is properly routed.

whenToCallPro: If all thermistors test good but tE persists.
videoSearchQuery: Kenmore LG dryer tE error thermistor fix

faq.q: How many thermistors does my dryer have?
faq.a: Most LG-manufactured dryers have two thermistors: one at the air inlet and one at the exhaust. Check both.

faq.q: Is tE dangerous?
faq.a: The dryer stops operation when tE appears, preventing any unsafe heating condition. Fix promptly to resume safe drying.

faq.q: Can I test thermistors with a basic multimeter?
faq.a: Yes. Set the multimeter to the 20K ohm range and measure across the thermistor terminals. A room-temperature reading of about 10K-12K ohms is normal.
